name: Fragment-Based Drug Discovery
description: Fragment-based discovery screens a library of small fragments by biophysical methods, confirms weak binders and their binding site by structure, then grows or links fragments through iterative medicinal chemistry to build a high-affinity lead.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Fragment library"]
    N2["Biophysical screen (SPR/NMR)"]
    N3{"Weak binders found?"}
    N4["Expand / rescreen"]
    N5["Confirm site by X-ray"]
    N6["Fragment growing"]
    N7["Fragment linking/merging"]
    N8{"Affinity improved?"}
    N9["Iterate chemistry"]
    N10["Optimized lead"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|no| N4
    N4 -->|rescreen| N2
    N3 -->|yes| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|grow more| N6
    N8 -->|yes| N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Discovering high-affinity ligands for proteins: SAR by NMR
sources.authors: Shuker, S. B.; Hajduk, P. J.; Meadows, R. P.; Fesik, S. W.

sources.title: Twenty years on: the impact of fragments on drug discovery
sources.authors: Erlanson, D. A.; Fesik, S. W.; Hubbard, R. E.; Jahnke, W.; Jhoti, H.

sources.title: The rise of fragment-based drug discovery
sources.authors: Murray, C. W.; Rees, D. C.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
